title: Pre-registered NO-GO / alpha-decay stop rule (DSR + MinTRL + PBO)
source: GitHub repo scout (4 Haiku explorers) + quant-phd-advisor literature review, 2026-05-31. Conversation: 'search quant trading repositories'.
why: The team has concluded directional alpha on XAU is largely exhausted and values honest negatives, but there is no FORMAL pre-registered rule that converts 'we think it's dead' into an audit-defensible kill. quant-phd-advisor: DSR + Minimum Track Record Length + PBO are the correct instruments and the math is ALREADY in pipeline/metrics.py (psr_dsr.json). Only candidate in the signal group with both the correct instrument AND a conclusion already reached.
value_rationale: VERDICT: Adopt (clean). Near-zero new math; directly serves the honest-negative goal; makes the research-exit a first-class repeatable output.
scope: Pre-register, before each audit: reject if DSR < target confidence (e.g. 0.95) vs an SR0 baseline encoding N trials, or if T < MinTRL for observed SR
CRITICAL: N must count ALL trials honestly (every primary, barrier sweep, feature-set variation across the whole program) — under-counting N re-inflates DSR
Wire the threshold check + pre-registered N into the report; gate the verdict on it
recommendation: Adopt. Extend pipeline/metrics.py + the proposal-audit report path with the pre-registered NO-GO gate.
out_of_scope: Not a substitute for fixing fold-starvation upstream — it makes the EXIT honest, not the gate pass.

external_refs.label: Deflated Sharpe Ratio (Bailey & Lopez de Prado 2014)
